name: Ration Card
issuingAuthority: Civil Supplies Department, Government of Telangana

sections.purpose: A Ration Card is an official document issued by the Civil Supplies Department that entitles a household to purchase subsidised food grains (rice, wheat, sugar, kerosene) from Fair Price Shops under the Public Distribution System (PDS). It also serves as: (1) Proof of family composition and address. (2) Identity proof for various government services. (3) Eligibility proof for BPL/AAY welfare schemes. (4) Supporting document for other certificates (income, caste, residence).
sections.eligibility: Must be a resident of Telangana. Household must not hold a ration card in any other state. Eligibility depends on card category: AAY (Antyodaya Anna Yojana) — poorest of the poor families. BPL (Below Poverty Line) — families below the state poverty threshold. APL (Above Poverty Line) — families above BPL but eligible for subsidised grains. Income criteria and family size determine category assignment.
documents: Aadhaar cards of all family members to be enrolled
Address proof (Electricity bill / Rent agreement / Voter ID)
Passport-size photographs of head of family and all members
Existing ration card surrender proof (if migrating from another state)
Gas connection slip or cylinder number (if applicable)
sections.fees: New ration card: approximately ₹10 to ₹35 at MeeSeva. Member addition/deletion: ₹10 to ₹25. Duplicate card: ₹25 to ₹50. Address change: ₹10 to ₹25.
sections.processingTime: New ration card: 30 to 45 days (includes field verification). Member addition/deletion: 15 to 30 days. Address change: 15 to 30 days. Duplicate card: 15 to 20 days.
sections.validity: Ration cards do not expire. However, they must be updated when: family composition changes (birth, death, marriage, separation), address changes, or during government-mandated re-verification drives.
applicationSteps: Step 1 — Gather Aadhaar cards of all family members
Step 2 — Visit Civil Supplies office or nearest MeeSeva centre
Step 3 — Submit application form with family details and photographs
Step 5 — Field verification conducted by Civil Supplies inspector at your address
Step 6 — Card issued after verification and approval (30–45 days)
Step 7 — Link Aadhaar of all members for e-POS (electronic point of sale) authentication at fair price shops
sections.memberAddition: To add a family member (new child, spouse after marriage): Submit application at Civil Supplies office or MeeSeva with: birth certificate (for child), marriage certificate (for spouse), member's Aadhaar card, and passport photo. Processing: 15–30 days.
sections.memberDeletion: To remove a member (death, separation, married and moved out): Submit application with: death certificate (for deceased), or written declaration for separation, and existing ration card. Processing: 15–30 days.
sections.addressChange: To change address on ration card: Submit application with: new address proof (Aadhaar/utility bill), existing ration card, and fee. If moving between districts, surrender old card and apply fresh in new district. Processing: 15–30 days.
sections.lostCard: If ration card is lost: Submit application for duplicate card with: FIR or self-declaration of loss, Aadhaar of family head, passport photo, and fee (₹25–₹50). Processing: 15–20 days.
commonMistakes: Not enrolling all family members at application time
Duplicate card in another state not surrendered — causes rejection
Wrong income category selected (APL vs BPL)
Aadhaar not linked for all members — prevents e-POS authentication at shops
Not updating card after family composition changes
rejectionReasons: Duplicate ration card detected in another state or district
Income/category mismatch (claiming BPL with above-threshold income)

edgeCases.title: Family has ration card in another state
edgeCases.problem: Family migrated to Telangana but still holds ration card in their previous state.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Surrender the old ration card at the Civil Supplies office of the previous state. Obtain a surrender certificate. Apply for new Telangana ration card with the surrender certificate. Without surrender, the system will flag duplication and reject.
edgeCases.notes: ONORC (One Nation One Ration Card) allows temporary purchase in any state but permanent card transfer requires surrender of old card.

faqs.question: What is a ration card?
faqs.answer: A Ration Card is an official document issued by the Civil Supplies Department of Telangana that entitles a household to buy subsidised food grains (rice, wheat, sugar) from Fair Price Shops under PDS. It also serves as identity, address, and family composition proof.

faqs.question: How to apply for new ration card?
faqs.answer: Visit Civil Supplies office or MeeSeva centre. Submit application with Aadhaar cards of all family members, address proof, photographs, and fee (₹10–₹35). Field verification is conducted. Card issued in 30–45 days.

faqs.question: What documents needed for ration card?
faqs.answer: Required: Application form, Aadhaar cards of all family members, address proof, income proof (for BPL), photographs of all members, gas connection details if applicable, and surrender proof if migrating from another state.

faqs.question: How much does ration card cost?
faqs.answer: New ration card: ₹10–₹35. Member addition/deletion: ₹10–₹25. Duplicate card: ₹25–₹50. Address change: ₹10–₹25.

faqs.question: How long does ration card take?
faqs.answer: New card: 30–45 days. Member changes: 15–30 days. Address change: 15–30 days. Duplicate card: 15–20 days.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for ration card?
faqs.answer: Any Telangana resident whose household does not hold a ration card in another state. Category depends on income: AAY (poorest), BPL (below poverty line), APL (above poverty line). All categories get subsidised grains at different rates.

faqs.question: How to add member to ration card?
faqs.answer: Submit application at Civil Supplies office or MeeSeva with: birth certificate (for child) or marriage certificate (for spouse), member's Aadhaar card, photograph, and existing ration card. Processing: 15–30 days.

faqs.question: How to remove member from ration card?
faqs.answer: Submit application with: death certificate (for deceased) or written declaration (for separation/married member moving out), existing ration card, and fee. Processing: 15–30 days.

faqs.question: What if I lost my ration card?
faqs.answer: Apply for duplicate card with: FIR or self-declaration of loss, Aadhaar of family head, passport photo, and fee (₹25–₹50). Processing: 15–20 days.

faqs.question: Does ration card expire?
faqs.answer: No. Ration cards do not expire. But they must be updated when family composition changes (birth, death, marriage) or during government re-verification drives.

faqs.question: Where to apply for ration card?
faqs.answer: Apply at Civil Supplies office of your mandal/district or any MeeSeva centre in Telangana. Helpline: 1800-425-0333. Portal: tgfood.gov.in.

faqs.question: How to change address on ration card?
faqs.answer: Submit application with new address proof, existing ration card, and fee. If moving between districts, surrender old card and apply fresh in new district. Processing: 15–30 days.

faqs.question: What mistakes to avoid for ration card?
faqs.answer: Avoid: not enrolling all family members, not surrendering card from another state, selecting wrong category, not linking Aadhaar for e-POS, and not updating after family changes.

faqs.question: What if my ration card is rejected?
faqs.answer: Common reasons: duplicate card in another state, address mismatch, wrong category, Aadhaar biometric errors, or missing photos. Resolve the issue and reapply with correct documentation.

faqs.question: Is Aadhaar needed for ration card?
faqs.answer: Yes. Aadhaar cards of ALL family members are mandatory. They are linked to the ration card for e-POS biometric authentication at fair price shops. Without Aadhaar linking, grains cannot be purchased.

faqs.question: Can I use ration card from another state in Telangana?
faqs.answer: Under ONORC (One Nation One Ration Card), you can temporarily purchase grains at any fair price shop in India using your existing card. But for a permanent Telangana card, you must surrender the old state card first and apply fresh.

faqs.question: What is BPL ration card?
faqs.answer: BPL (Below Poverty Line) ration card is issued to families whose income is below the state poverty threshold. BPL cardholders receive food grains at heavily subsidised rates (e.g., rice at ₹1/kg in Telangana). Income certificate may be required to prove BPL status.

faqs.question: Can I track ration card application?
faqs.answer: Track at epds.telangana.gov.in or tgfood.gov.in using your application reference number. You can also visit the Civil Supplies office or call helpline 1800-425-0333.

faqs.question: What grains can I get with ration card?
faqs.answer: Telangana PDS provides: Rice (at ₹1/kg for eligible households), Wheat, Sugar, and Kerosene (availability varies). Quantities depend on card category and number of family members. Purchase via e-POS biometric authentication at designated Fair Price Shop.

faqs.question: How to link Aadhaar with ration card?
faqs.answer: Visit your assigned Fair Price Shop or Civil Supplies office with Aadhaar cards of all family members and existing ration card. Biometric verification (fingerprint) is done to link each member. Without linking, e-POS authentication fails and grains cannot be purchased.
